content: Most existing discussions frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective iterative improvement workflow, but it can be reframed as a base framework that can be augmented by multiple inconsistent perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to solve the common problem of blind spots that single-perspective self-feedback cannot resolve. This creates a hybrid framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional training or labeled data while fixing its key limitation of repeated self-confirmation of biased initial outputs.
